description: Jo Churchill was Minister of State for the Department for Work and Pensions from 13 November 2023 to 5 July 2024. She was previously Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip) from 7 September 2022 to 13 November 2023. She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs from 17 September 2021 to 6 July 2022. She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department of Health and Social Care from 26 July 2019 to 16 September 2021. Before that, she was Assistant Government Whip from 9 January 2018 to 26 July 2019.

details.body:
Jo Churchill was Minister of State for the Department for Work and Pensions from 13 November 2023 to 5 July 2024.

She was previously Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip) from 7 September 2022 to 13 November 2023.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs from 17 September 2021 to 6 July 2022.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department of Health and Social Care from 26 July 2019 to 16 September 2021. Before that, she was Assistant Government Whip from 9 January 2018 to 26 July 2019.

Jo Churchill was Minister of State for the Department for Work and Pensions from 13 November 2023 to 5 July 2024.

She was previously Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip) from 7 September 2022 to 13 November 2023.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs from 17 September 2021 to 6 July 2022.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department of Health and Social Care from 26 July 2019 to 16 September 2021. Before that, she was Assistant Government Whip from 9 January 2018 to 26 July 2019.

• Labour Market – including employer engagement 

  • Jobcentre Plus/National Jobs and Careers Service reform 

  • poverty 

  • addressing economic inactivity 

  • locally led employment support 

  • Skills and In Work Progression 

  • Occupational Health and Statutory Sick Pay 

  • Childcare and Parental Employment 

  • Youth Guarantee 

  • Disability employment

  • Conditionality/sanctions

Jo Churchill was Minister of State for the Department for Work and Pensions from 13 November 2023 to 5 July 2024.

She was previously Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip) from 7 September 2022 to 13 November 2023.

role.title: Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip)

role_appointments.title: Jo Churchill MP - Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip)

details.body:
Jo Churchill was Minister of State for the Department for Work and Pensions from 13 November 2023 to 5 July 2024.

She was previously Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip) from 7 September 2022 to 13 November 2023.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs from 17 September 2021 to 6 July 2022.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department of Health and Social Care from 26 July 2019 to 16 September 2021. Before that, she was Assistant Government Whip from 9 January 2018 to 26 July 2019.

Responsibilities include:

  • science and innovation, including gene editing and agri-food innovation
  • Defra delivery of Net Zero
  • delivery of climate change adaptation across government
  • environmental management and regulation (chemicals, waste, air quality)
  • commercial projects
  • ministerial lead for COVID-19 relating to Defra issues
  • Commons Minister for animal welfare, including animal welfare legislation

ordered_parent_organisations.title: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

role.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Agri-Innovation and Climate Adaptation)

role_appointments.title: Jo Churchill MP - Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Agri-Innovation and Climate Adaptation)

details.body:
Jo Churchill was Minister of State for the Department for Work and Pensions from 13 November 2023 to 5 July 2024.

She was previously Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip) from 7 September 2022 to 13 November 2023.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs from 17 September 2021 to 6 July 2022.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department of Health and Social Care from 26 July 2019 to 16 September 2021. Before that, she was Assistant Government Whip from 9 January 2018 to 26 July 2019.

The Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Vaccines and Public Health leads on the following policy areas:

  • COVID-19 vaccines
  • health improvement
  • levelling up
  • prevention
  • immunisation and screening
  • UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA):
    • Test and Trace
    • emergency preparedness
    • infectious diseases
    • environmental health
  • abortion
  • global health security
  • lead minister for crisis response
  • sponsorship of PHE (until Oct 2021), FSA, UKHSA

details.body:
Jo Churchill was Minister of State for the Department for Work and Pensions from 13 November 2023 to 5 July 2024.

She was previously Vice Chamberlain of HM Household (Government Whip) from 7 September 2022 to 13 November 2023.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs from 17 September 2021 to 6 July 2022.

She was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department of Health and Social Care from 26 July 2019 to 16 September 2021. Before that, she was Assistant Government Whip from 9 January 2018 to 26 July 2019.

Whips are MPs or Lords appointed by each party to help organise their party’s contribution to parliamentary business. They are responsible for making sure the maximum number of their party members vote, and vote the way their party wants.

title: Jo Churchill
